        <!-- BOTTOM: blocked - no system changes -->
        <g transform="translate(420,250)">
          <line x1="40" y1="0" x2="40" y2="40" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="20" x2="60" y2="60" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round"/>
          <line x1="60" y1="20" x2="20" y2="60" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round"/>
          <text x="40" y="84" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="8" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">NO SYSTEM CHANGES</text>
        </g>

      </svg>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">The scan reads from your PC, scores what it finds, and writes one text report to your Desktop. <strong>Nothing leaves the machine.</strong> The script doesn't open a network connection, doesn't download or install anything, and doesn't change a single system setting. It runs once, writes the report, and exits.</p>
  </section>

  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <!-- TWO-COLUMN: does / does not -->
  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">The specifics</div>
    <h2>Exactly what it does and exactly what it doesn't</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="split">
      <div class="panel does">
        <div class="panel-label">What it does</div>
        <h3>Read, score, write one file</h3>
        <ul>
          <li>Reads your running process list (same data Task Manager shows)</li>
          <li>Reads your installed-software list (same data the Settings app shows)</li>
          <li>Reads USB device history (which devices have been plugged in over time)</li>
          <li>Reads file names and paths only &mdash; <strong>never opens the inside of any file</strong></li>
          <li>Compares what it finds against a list of known cheat / input-device names</li>
          <li>Writes one .txt file to your Desktop with a timestamped name</li>
          <li>Exits</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
      <div class="panel doesnt">
        <div class="panel-label">What it doesn't do</div>
        <h3>None of the things you'd reasonably worry about</h3>
        <ul>
          <li>Makes <strong>one opt-in</strong> outbound call &mdash; the LOLDrivers (loldrivers.io) BYOVD cross-reference &mdash; which prompts <em>Y/N</em> before running and is the only <code>Invoke-WebRequest</code> in the kit. No telemetry, no phone-home, no cloud upload. Nothing about your PC is sent &mdash; only the public driver database is downloaded.</li>
          <li>Doesn't download or install anything</li>
          <li>Doesn't modify any existing file, registry key, service, or setting</li>
          <li>Doesn't read the contents of your photos, documents, messages, or saved passwords</li>
          <li>Doesn't run in the background after it finishes &mdash; no scheduled task, no startup entry, no service</li>
          <li>Doesn't send the report anywhere automatically. It just sits on your Desktop until you choose to send it</li>
          <li>Doesn't require any external software, downloads, or accounts</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <!-- VERIFY -->
  <!-- ============================================================ -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Don't take my word for it</div>
    <h2>Three ways to verify any of this yourself</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="verify-list">
      <div class="verify-card">
        <div class="num">VERIFY 01</div>
        <h4>Open the script in Notepad</h4>
        <p>Right-click <code>forensic-scan.ps1</code> &rarr; Open with &rarr; Notepad. Every operation the script performs is visible as plain English plus PowerShell. Search <code>scanner\*.ps1</code> for <code>Invoke-Web</code> and you'll find <strong>exactly one</strong> result &mdash; the opt-in LOLDrivers fetch in <code>forensic-common.ps1</code>, prompted with Y/N at runtime. Search for <code>Remove-</code> or <code>Set-Item</code> and you'll find none in the scan path.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="verify-card">
        <div class="num">VERIFY 02</div>
        <h4>Watch network activity while it runs</h4>
        <p>Open Task Manager &rarr; Performance &rarr; Ethernet / Wi-Fi. Start the scan. If you press anything other than Y at the LOLDrivers prompt, the "Send" graph stays at zero kbps for the entire run. If you press Y, you'll see a single small fetch (~50 KB) from loldrivers.io and then nothing else. There are no other outbound connections.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="verify-card">
        <div class="num">VERIFY 03</div>
        <h4>Look at the output yourself</h4>
        <p>The .txt report on the Desktop is the only thing the scan creates. Open it in Notepad. Every line is human-readable. There is no hidden second file anywhere on the system.</p>
      </div>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">The scan is roughly 950 lines of PowerShell. The whole script is plain text in a single file you can read top to bottom. There is no compiled binary, no DLL, no installer, no obfuscation. <strong>If something the script does isn't visible in the script, the script doesn't do it.</strong></p>
  </section>

  <footer>
    <span>Alibi &middot; Safety Card &middot; by Bread, with Drownmw</span>
    <span>alibi-safety-card.html</span>
  </footer>

</div>
</body>
</html>

```

<div class="page">

  <div class="eyebrow">Alibi &middot; Console Companion</div>
  <h1 class="title">Why a <em>USB stick</em> can't scan your console</h1>
  <p class="subtitle">A picture-first explainer for the very reasonable question: "If a flash drive can scan a PC, can't we just plug one into the PS5 or Xbox?" The answer is no &mdash; and the reason is the same reason console cheating works the way it does.</p>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 1: HERO - the question and the no -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">The 30-second version</div>
    <h2>The answer in one picture</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 960 280" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;max-width:960px">
        <!-- LEFT: USB stick with scanner files -->
        <g transform="translate(40,80)">
          <!-- USB stick body -->
          <rect x="0" y="20" width="160" height="60" rx="6" fill="#2E3A4A" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <rect x="160" y="32" width="40" height="36" fill="#C9A961"/>
          <rect x="166" y="40" width="4" height="20" fill="#8A6914"/>
          <rect x="176" y="40" width="4" height="20" fill="#8A6914"/>
          <rect x="186" y="40" width="4" height="20" fill="#8A6914"/>
          <text x="80" y="56" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="10" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">SCANNER FILES</text>
          <text x="100" y="120" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="10" fill="#4A463E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">USB FLASH DRIVE</text>
          <text x="100" y="135" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="11" fill="#8A857C" text-anchor="middle">what you'd want to plug in</text>
        </g>

        <g transform="translate(420,100)">
          <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="60" y2="60" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
          <line x1="60" y1="0" x2="0" y2="60" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>
          <text x="30" y="92" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="11" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700" letter-spacing="1.5">CAN'T WORK</text>
        </g>

        <!-- RIGHT: Console -->
        <g transform="translate(580,40)">
          <!-- Console body (PS5-ish abstract) -->
          <rect x="0" y="20" width="280" height="160" rx="8" fill="#3A3530" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
          <rect x="20" y="40" width="240" height="120" rx="3" fill="#2A2520"/>
          <!-- "lock" icon on front -->
          <g transform="translate(125,75)">
            <rect x="0" y="10" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#C9A961" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>

            <circle cx="15" cy="21" r="3" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
          </g>
          <!-- USB port (small) -->
          <rect x="50" y="170" width="20" height="6" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
          <rect x="80" y="170" width="20" height="6" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
          <text x="140" y="200" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="10" fill="#4A463E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">PS5 / XBOX</text>
          <text x="140" y="216" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="11" fill="#8A857C" text-anchor="middle">a sealed box - locked by design</text>
        </g>
      </svg>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">You can plug a flash drive into a PS5 or Xbox port, but the console will not run any "scanner program" stored on it. <strong>Consoles are sealed boxes by design.</strong> Only software signed by Sony or Microsoft is allowed to run. Everything else &mdash; including helpful tools meant to prove the console is clean &mdash; is rejected at the door.</p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 2: SEALED BOX METAPHOR -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Step 1 &middot; What you can put on a flash drive</div>
    <h2>The console accepts a few file types. Programs are not one of them.</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="viz tall">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 960 220" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;max-width:960px">
        <!-- Sealed console as a vault -->
        <g transform="translate(360,30)">
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="240" height="160" rx="6" fill="#3A3530" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
          <!-- big lock -->
          <g transform="translate(95,40)">
            <rect x="0" y="20" width="50" height="40" rx="3" fill="#C9A961" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
            <path d="M 10 20 L 10 10 Q 10 -2 25 -2 Q 40 -2 40 10 L 40 20" fill="none" stroke="#C9A961" stroke-width="5"/>

            <rect x="22" y="42" width="6" height="10" fill="#1A1A1A"/>

            <rect x="0" y="0" width="30" height="36" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
            <text x="15" y="22" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">.exe</text>
            <line x1="-5" y1="-5" x2="35" y2="40" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2"/>
            <text x="60" y="22" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="12" fill="#6B1E1E">any program</text>
          </g>
          <g transform="translate(0,106)">
            <rect x="0" y="0" width="30" height="36" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
            <text x="15" y="22" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">.bat</text>
            <line x1="-5" y1="-5" x2="35" y2="40" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2"/>
            <text x="60" y="22" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="12" fill="#6B1E1E">batch file</text>
          </g>
          <g transform="translate(0,152)">
            <rect x="0" y="0" width="30" height="36" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
            <text x="15" y="22" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">.pkg</text>
            <line x1="-5" y1="-5" x2="35" y2="40" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2"/>
            <text x="60" y="22" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="12" fill="#6B1E1E">unsigned installer</text>
          </g>
        </g>

        <!-- arrows getting bounced back (rejected) -->
        <g stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none">
          <path d="M 610 34 L 745 34" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
          <polygon points="610,30 602,34 610,38" fill="#6B1E1E" stroke="none"/>
          <path d="M 610 80 L 745 80" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
          <polygon points="610,76 602,80 610,84" fill="#6B1E1E" stroke="none"/>
          <path d="M 610 126 L 745 126" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
          <polygon points="610,122 602,126 610,130" fill="#6B1E1E" stroke="none"/>
          <path d="M 610 172 L 745 172" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
          <polygon points="610,168 602,172 610,176" fill="#6B1E1E" stroke="none"/>
        </g>
      </svg>
    </div>

    <div class="mailroom">
      <div class="mailroom-col accepted">
        <div class="col-label">What the console will read off a USB</div>
        <h3>Files for built-in apps only</h3>
        <ul>
          <li>Photos (in the Media app)</li>
          <li>Music and video (in the Media app)</li>
          <li>Game save backups (per-game, encrypted)</li>
          <li>System updates &mdash; <strong>only if</strong> they're signed by Sony or Microsoft</li>
          <li>Extra game storage (after the console reformats the drive itself)</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
      <div class="mailroom-col rejected">
        <div class="col-label">What the console will refuse to run</div>
        <h3>Anything that looks like a program</h3>
        <ul>
          <li><code>.exe</code> &mdash; Windows programs</li>
          <li><code>.ps1</code> / <code>.bat</code> &mdash; scripts of any kind</li>
          <li><code>.pkg</code>, <code>.app</code>, <code>.nsp</code> &mdash; unsigned installers</li>
          <li>HTML files (no built-in browser to open them in)</li>
          <li>Anything that isn't on the "accepted" list to the left</li>
        </ul>
      </div>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">Think of the console like a vault with a tiny mail slot. The slot only fits certain envelopes. Photos and music can slide through. Programs of any kind &mdash; including helpful ones &mdash; cannot fit. <strong>There is no path through the slot to make the vault run code it didn't come with.</strong></p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 3: SIGNED SOFTWARE METAPHOR -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Step 2 &middot; Why even "good" programs can't run</div>
    <h2>Only software stamped by the manufacturer is allowed inside</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 960 260" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;max-width:960px">
        <!-- LEFT: signed document accepted -->
        <g transform="translate(60,40)">
          <text x="120" y="-12" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="10" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.5" font-weight="600">ACCEPTED</text>
          <!-- Document with seal -->
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="170" height="180" rx="3" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="30" x2="150" y2="30" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="50" x2="130" y2="50" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="68" x2="150" y2="68" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="86" x2="120" y2="86" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="104" x2="140" y2="104" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="122" x2="110" y2="122" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <!-- wax seal -->
          <circle cx="120" cy="155" r="22" fill="#6B1E1E" stroke="#4A1010" stroke-width="2"/>
          <text x="120" y="153" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700">SIGNED</text>
          <text x="120" y="163" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="700">BY SONY</text>
          <text x="85" y="200" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="11" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle">a game from PlayStation Store,</text>
          <text x="85" y="215" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="11" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle">an official system update</text>
        </g>

        <!-- ARROW IN -->
        <g transform="translate(280,120)">
          <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="60" y2="0" stroke="#1F3F2E" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
          <polygon points="60,-5 70,0 60,5" fill="#1F3F2E"/>
        </g>

        <!-- CENTER: the vault -->
        <g transform="translate(370,40)">
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="220" height="180" rx="6" fill="#3A3530" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
          <g transform="translate(85,55)">
            <rect x="0" y="20" width="50" height="40" rx="3" fill="#C9A961" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
            <path d="M 10 20 L 10 10 Q 10 -2 25 -2 Q 40 -2 40 10 L 40 20" fill="none" stroke="#C9A961" stroke-width="5"/>
            <circle cx="25" cy="40" r="4" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
          </g>
          <text x="110" y="200" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="10" fill="#4A463E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.5" font-weight="600">THE CONSOLE</text>
        </g>

        <!-- ARROW BLOCKED -->
        <g transform="translate(630,120)">
          <line x1="0" y1="0" x2="60" y2="0" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="4,3"/>
          <polygon points="0,-5 -10,0 0,5" fill="#6B1E1E"/>
          <text x="30" y="-12" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="8" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1" font-weight="600">BOUNCED</text>
        </g>

        <!-- RIGHT: unsigned document rejected -->
        <g transform="translate(720,40)">
          <text x="100" y="-12" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="10" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.5" font-weight="600">REJECTED</text>
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="170" height="180" rx="3" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="30" x2="150" y2="30" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="50" x2="130" y2="50" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="68" x2="150" y2="68" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="86" x2="120" y2="86" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="104" x2="140" y2="104" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="20" y1="122" x2="110" y2="122" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="1"/>
          <!-- "no seal" placeholder -->
          <circle cx="120" cy="155" r="22" fill="none" stroke="#B8B0A0" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
          <text x="120" y="158" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="8" fill="#B8B0A0" text-anchor="middle">NO SEAL</text>
          <text x="85" y="200" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="11" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">a cheat program,</text>
          <text x="85" y="215" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="11" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">a forensic scanner, anything else</text>
        </g>
      </svg>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">Every piece of software for a PS5 or Xbox carries a cryptographic "stamp" proving it came from Sony or Microsoft. The console checks every program for this stamp before running it. No stamp, no execution. <strong>This is what makes consoles harder to hack than PCs &mdash; and what makes a third-party scanner impossible.</strong> The lock doesn't know whether the program in front of it is a cheat or a scanner. It only knows whether the stamp is there.</p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 4: SAME LOCK, BOTH DIRECTIONS -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Step 3 &middot; The trade-off cuts both ways</div>
    <h2>The same lock that keeps cheats out, keeps scanners out</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="viz">
      <div class="symmetric">
        <div class="sym-panel">
          <div class="sym-tag">If you're a cheater</div>
          <svg width="120" height="120" viewBox="0 0 120 120">
            <!-- Skull/cheat icon attempting to enter -->
            <circle cx="60" cy="45" r="22" fill="#6B1E1E"/>
            <circle cx="52" cy="42" r="3" fill="#FBF9F4"/>
            <circle cx="68" cy="42" r="3" fill="#FBF9F4"/>
            <rect x="55" y="50" width="10" height="3" fill="#FBF9F4"/>
            <!-- arrow pointing into a wall -->
            <line x1="60" y1="70" x2="60" y2="92" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
            <polygon points="55,90 60,100 65,90" fill="#6B1E1E"/>
            <!-- wall/lock -->
            <rect x="20" y="100" width="80" height="16" rx="2" fill="#3A3530"/>
            <rect x="55" y="103" width="10" height="10" fill="#C9A961"/>
          </svg>
          <h3>Cheat software can't run on the console</h3>
          <p class="sym-text">No <code style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:.86rem">.exe</code> aimbot, no memory-editing tool, no homebrew cheat can be installed on a stock PS5 or Xbox. The lock catches all of it. <strong>This is good.</strong></p>
        </div>
        <div class="sym-panel">
          <div class="sym-tag">If you're trying to prove innocence</div>
          <svg width="120" height="120" viewBox="0 0 120 120">
            <!-- Magnifying glass attempting to enter -->
            <circle cx="50" cy="35" r="20" fill="none" stroke="#2E3A4A" stroke-width="3.5"/>
            <line x1="64" y1="49" x2="78" y2="63" stroke="#2E3A4A" stroke-width="3.5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
            <!-- arrow pointing into wall -->
            <line x1="60" y1="72" x2="60" y2="92" stroke="#2E3A4A" stroke-width="2.5" stroke-dasharray="3,3"/>
            <polygon points="55,90 60,100 65,90" fill="#2E3A4A"/>
            <!-- wall/lock -->
            <rect x="20" y="100" width="80" height="16" rx="2" fill="#3A3530"/>
            <rect x="55" y="103" width="10" height="10" fill="#C9A961"/>
          </svg>
          <h3>Scanner software can't run on the console either</h3>
          <p class="sym-text">No third-party forensic tool, no system audit, no verification script can run on the console &mdash; not even a friendly one designed to prove the box is clean. The lock catches it the same way. <strong>This is the catch.</strong></p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">The lock doesn't read intent. It only reads the stamp. Sony's stamp opens the door; nothing else does. That's why we have to verify the console <strong>from outside</strong> instead of from inside &mdash; the same lock blocks both the bad actor and the auditor.</p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 5: HOW CHEATS WRAP THE BOX -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Step 4 &middot; What cheaters do instead</div>
    <h2>If you can't get into the console, you wrap it from outside</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="viz tall">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 960 380" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;max-width:960px">
        <!-- console at center, untouched -->
        <g transform="translate(410,140)">
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="140" height="100" rx="5" fill="#3A3530" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
          <g transform="translate(50,30)">
            <rect x="0" y="15" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#C9A961" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>
            <path d="M 6 15 L 6 8 Q 6 0 15 0 Q 24 0 24 8 L 24 15" fill="none" stroke="#C9A961" stroke-width="3"/>
          </g>
          <text x="70" y="120" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#4A463E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">CONSOLE</text>
          <text x="70" y="134" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#8A857C" text-anchor="middle">untouched, still locked</text>
        </g>

        <!-- Wrapper 1: HDMI to capture card to aimbot PC -->
        <g transform="translate(560,80)">
          <!-- HDMI line out -->
          <line x1="0" y1="80" x2="40" y2="80" stroke="#2A2520" stroke-width="3"/>
          <!-- capture card -->
          <rect x="40" y="62" width="80" height="36" fill="#1F3F2E" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>

          <line x1="120" y1="80" x2="160" y2="80" stroke="#2A2520" stroke-width="3"/>
          <!-- aimbot PC -->
          <rect x="160" y="40" width="100" height="80" rx="3" fill="#6B1E1E" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <text x="210" y="72" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">VISION</text>
          <text x="210" y="86" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">AIMBOT</text>
          <text x="210" y="100" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="8" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0.8">PC</text>
          <text x="130" y="20" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600" letter-spacing="1">WRAPPER 1</text>
          <text x="130" y="146" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">video feed &rarr; AI watches</text>
        </g>

        <!-- Wrapper 2: Controller adapter on USB -->
        <g transform="translate(540,260)">
          <line x1="20" y1="20" x2="60" y2="20" stroke="#2A2520" stroke-width="3"/>
          <rect x="60" y="0" width="100" height="40" fill="#1F3F2E" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <text x="110" y="24" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="8" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">XIM / CRONUS</text>
          <line x1="160" y1="20" x2="200" y2="20" stroke="#2A2520" stroke-width="3"/>
          <!-- Mouse + keyboard -->
          <ellipse cx="220" cy="20" rx="14" ry="20" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#2A2520" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <rect x="244" y="6" width="36" height="26" rx="2" fill="#2A2520"/>
          <text x="60" y="-10" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" font-weight="600" letter-spacing="1">WRAPPER 2</text>
          <text x="120" y="60" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">mouse+keyboard &rarr; pretends to be a controller</text>
        </g>

        <!-- Wrapper 3: HDMI capture pass-through visible in the cable -->
        <g transform="translate(170,200)">
          <text x="120" y="0" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" font-weight="600" letter-spacing="1" text-anchor="middle">WRAPPER 3</text>
          <text x="120" y="14" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">the cable itself becomes the data feed</text>
        </g>

        <!-- Wrapper 4: Modified controller -->
        <g transform="translate(60,40)">
          <text x="90" y="-12" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" font-weight="600" letter-spacing="1" text-anchor="middle">WRAPPER 4</text>
          <!-- controller -->
          <path d="M 30 30 Q 30 0 60 0 L 120 0 Q 150 0 150 30 L 150 50 Q 150 60 140 60 L 100 60 L 90 70 L 80 60 L 40 60 Q 30 60 30 50 Z" fill="#3A3530" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <circle cx="55" cy="20" r="6" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
          <circle cx="125" cy="20" r="6" fill="#1A1A1A"/>
          <rect x="80" y="14" width="20" height="10" rx="2" fill="#6B1E1E"/>
          <text x="90" y="22" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="6" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">MOD</text>
          <text x="90" y="84" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">modified controller</text>
          <text x="90" y="96" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">with macro chip inside</text>
        </g>

        <!-- arrows from console outward (showing wrappers attach OUTSIDE) -->
        <g stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="1.5" fill="none" stroke-dasharray="3,3">
          <!-- top right to wrapper 1 -->
          <path d="M 550 160 L 600 140"/>
          <!-- bottom right to wrapper 2 -->
          <path d="M 550 240 L 600 280"/>
          <!-- left to wrapper 4 -->
          <path d="M 410 180 L 240 100"/>
        </g>
      </svg>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">Cheaters can't get into the console, so they build a ring of equipment <em>around</em> the console. A capture card peeks at the screen. A second PC watches the video and figures out where to aim. A controller adapter turns mouse-and-keyboard movements into controller input the console will accept. The console itself is never touched. <strong>The cheating happens entirely outside the locked box.</strong></p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 6: VERIFICATION MIRRORS THE WRAPPERS -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Step 5 &middot; How we verify, given the same constraint</div>
    <h2>We check the same wrappers the cheaters use</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="wrappers">
      <div class="wrapper-card verify">
        <div class="wrap-num">CHECKPOINT 1</div>
        <h3>Follow the HDMI cable from console to TV</h3>
        <p>A photo showing the cable goes <em>directly</em> from the console to the TV proves nothing is intercepting the video feed. If a capture device is in the path, the photo shows it. Either way, the truth is visible.</p>
        <span class="check">&check; Photo in the setup checklist</span>
      </div>
      <div class="wrapper-card verify">
        <div class="wrap-num">CHECKPOINT 2</div>
        <h3>Photograph every USB port on the console</h3>
        <p>An XIM, Cronus, or ReaSnow adapter is a small device plugged into a console USB port. A close-up photo of all the ports either shows one (evidence) or shows nothing (also evidence).</p>
        <span class="check">&check; Photos in the setup checklist</span>
      </div>
      <div class="wrapper-card verify">
        <div class="wrap-num">CHECKPOINT 3</div>
        <h3>If there's a nearby PC, scan it</h3>
        <p>The vision-aimbot software lives on a PC, not the console. If a PC is part of the gaming rig, the PC scanner finds the aimbot software or proves it isn't there. The console-rig audit tool does this.</p>
        <span class="check">&check; <code style="font-family:var(--mono);font-size:.78rem;color:var(--clean)">console-run-check.bat</code></span>
      </div>
      <div class="wrapper-card verify">
        <div class="wrap-num">CHECKPOINT 4</div>
        <h3>Photograph the controller itself</h3>
        <p>Photos of the outside (and, for high-stakes cases, the inside) of every controller used catch the modified-controller route. A genuine factory controller looks one way; a modded one looks different.</p>
        <span class="check">&check; Photos in the setup checklist</span>
      </div>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">For every place a cheater could wrap the console, there's a matching way to check from outside. The console is never touched, never scanned, never opened &mdash; <strong>the verification mirrors the cheat method exactly</strong>. This is the only honest way to do it given how locked-down the box is.</p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 7: WHAT THE FLASH DRIVE ACTUALLY CARRIES -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">Step 6 &middot; So what does a flash drive carry?</div>
    <h2>The kit, delivered. Used on a PC or a phone &mdash; not the console.</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="viz">
      <svg viewBox="0 0 960 240" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" style="width:100%;max-width:960px">
        <!-- USB stick -->
        <g transform="translate(40,80)">
          <rect x="0" y="20" width="140" height="50" rx="5" fill="#2E3A4A" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <rect x="140" y="30" width="36" height="30" fill="#C9A961"/>
          <text x="70" y="50" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">VERIFICATION KIT</text>
          <text x="88" y="105" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#4A463E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">USB FLASH DRIVE</text>
        </g>

        <!-- Split: GOES TO PC, NOT CONSOLE -->
        <g transform="translate(240,100)">
          <line x1="0" y1="10" x2="50" y2="-40" stroke="#1F3F2E" stroke-width="2.5"/>
          <polygon points="46,-44 56,-40 50,-32" fill="#1F3F2E"/>
          <line x1="0" y1="10" x2="50" y2="60" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2.5"/>
          <line x1="46" y1="56" x2="56" y2="64" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2.5"/>
          <line x1="46" y1="64" x2="56" y2="56" stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="2.5"/>
        </g>

        <!-- TOP RIGHT: PC -->
        <g transform="translate(350,30)">
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="160" height="100" rx="3" fill="#1A1A1A" stroke="#3A3530" stroke-width="2"/>
          <rect x="6" y="6" width="148" height="84" fill="#2A2520"/>
          <text x="80" y="50" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="11" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">A PC</text>
          <text x="80" y="68" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="10" fill="#FBF9F4" text-anchor="middle" opacity="0.8">where the scanner runs</text>
          <text x="80" y="125" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">USE THE KIT HERE</text>
        </g>

        <!-- BOTTOM RIGHT: phone (for the checklist) -->
        <g transform="translate(550,40)">
          <rect x="0" y="0" width="60" height="110" rx="6" fill="#1A1A1A" stroke="#3A3530" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <rect x="4" y="10" width="52" height="92" fill="#FBF9F4"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="22" x2="46" y2="22" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="1"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="32" x2="42" y2="32" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="0.8"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="42" x2="46" y2="42" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="0.8"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="52" x2="40" y2="52" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="0.8"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="62" x2="44" y2="62" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="0.8"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="72" x2="38" y2="72" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="0.8"/>
          <line x1="14" y1="82" x2="42" y2="82" stroke="#8A857C" stroke-width="0.8"/>
          <text x="30" y="125" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">OR A PHONE</text>
          <text x="30" y="140" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="9" fill="#8A857C" text-anchor="middle">for the photo checklist</text>
        </g>

        <!-- ALSO BOTTOM: console with big NO sign -->
        <g transform="translate(680,40)">
          <rect x="0" y="20" width="200" height="100" rx="5" fill="#3A3530" stroke="#1A1A1A" stroke-width="2"/>
          <rect x="10" y="30" width="180" height="80" rx="2" fill="#2A2520"/>
          <g transform="translate(85,55)">
            <rect x="0" y="15" width="30" height="22" rx="2" fill="#C9A961"/>
            <path d="M 6 15 L 6 8 Q 6 0 15 0 Q 24 0 24 8 L 24 15" fill="none" stroke="#C9A961" stroke-width="3"/>
          </g>
          <!-- big red X over it -->
          <g stroke="#6B1E1E" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round">
            <line x1="20" y1="40" x2="180" y2="100"/>
            <line x1="180" y1="40" x2="20" y2="100"/>
          </g>
          <text x="100" y="140" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" letter-spacing="1.2" font-weight="600">NEVER THE CONSOLE</text>
          <text x="100" y="156" font-family="Newsreader" font-style="italic" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle">it wouldn't run the files anyway</text>
        </g>
      </svg>
    </div>

    <div class="kit-row">
      <div class="kit-card">
        <svg width="64" height="80" viewBox="0 0 64 80">
          <path d="M 8 0 L 44 0 L 60 16 L 60 80 L 8 80 Z" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="2"/>
          <path d="M 44 0 L 44 16 L 60 16" fill="none" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="2"/>
          <text x="34" y="44" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">CHECK</text>
          <text x="34" y="55" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="7" fill="#1F3F2E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">LIST</text>
        </svg>
        <div class="kit-name">setup-checklist.html</div>
        <div class="kit-desc">Visual photo guide. Open on a PC or read on a phone.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="kit-card">
        <svg width="64" height="80" viewBox="0 0 64 80">
          <path d="M 8 0 L 44 0 L 60 16 L 60 80 L 8 80 Z" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="2"/>
          <path d="M 44 0 L 44 16 L 60 16" fill="none" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="2"/>
          <text x="34" y="44" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="9" fill="#6B1E1E" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="600">.bat</text>
          <text x="34" y="58" font-family="IBM Plex Mono" font-size="6" fill="#1A1A1A" text-anchor="middle">SCANNER</text>
        </svg>
        <div class="kit-name">console-run-check.bat</div>
        <div class="kit-desc">PC-side scanner. Runs on the nearby PC if you have one.</div>
      </div>
      <div class="kit-card">
        <svg width="64" height="80" viewBox="0 0 64 80">
          <path d="M 8 0 L 44 0 L 60 16 L 60 80 L 8 80 Z" fill="#FBF9F4" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="2"/>
          <path d="M 44 0 L 44 16 L 60 16" fill="none" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="2"/>
          <line x1="16" y1="40" x2="52" y2="40" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <line x1="16" y1="50" x2="48" y2="50" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
          <line x1="16" y1="60" x2="44" y2="60" stroke="#4A463E" stroke-width="1.5"/>
        </svg>
        <div class="kit-name">README.txt</div>
        <div class="kit-desc">Tells you which file to use given your setup.</div>
      </div>
    </div>

    <p class="caption">The flash drive carries the <strong>kit</strong>, not a console scanner. The kit gets used on a PC (to run the scan) or a phone (to read the checklist). The flash drive never has to enter the console at all. That's the workaround for the lock &mdash; we don't try to break it.</p>
  </section>

  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <!-- SECTION 8: HONEST LIMITS -->
  <!-- ====================================================================== -->
  <section>
    <div class="section-num">What this approach can and can't do</div>
    <h2>The honest limits</h2>
    <div class="section-rule"></div>

    <div class="endnote">
      <h3>Verification from outside is never as strong as verification from inside.</h3>
      <p>If we could scan the PS5 itself, the way we scan a PC, the answer would be cut-and-dried. Photos and PC-side scans <strong>raise the cost of cheating undetected</strong>, but a determined person can stage some of it &mdash; remove the capture card for the photo, swap controllers between takes, unplug the adapter mid-shoot.</p>
      <p>The strongest version of outside-verification is <strong>live, on video</strong>: the player walks through the rig in real time while a reviewer watches. Static photos and scan reports can be staged; a live walkthrough can't be rehearsed without the reviewer noticing.</p>
      <p>This is the architectural reality. The locked-down console is good for the gaming ecosystem overall &mdash; it stops most cheats by existing &mdash; but it forces verification of an individual rig to happen from the outside in. The same constraint that makes consoles harder to cheat on also makes them harder to audit.</p>
    </div>
  </section>

  <footer>
    <span>Alibi &middot; Console Lockdown Explainer &middot; by Bread, with Drownmw</span>
    <span>console-lockdown-explained.html</span>
  </footer>

</div>
</body>
</html>

```

`alibi-engine/scanner/console-rig-audit.ps1`:

```ps1
<#
.SYNOPSIS
    Alibi (console-rig mode) v1.1 - scans a PC sitting next to a console for
    cheat-MITM software stacks.

.DESCRIPTION
    Use case: a console player wants to demonstrate that the PC connected
    to their console rig (capture-card host, second monitor, streaming PC,
    or shared desktop) is NOT being used to help them cheat at console
    games. They run this script, send the report to a reviewer.

    The console itself is a black box - this script cannot scan it. It can
    only scan the Windows PC that is part of the gaming setup. If there is
    no PC in the loop, this script is not the right tool - use the visual
    setup checklist (console-setup-checklist.html) instead.

    This is the CONSOLE-MODE driver. The actual scanning logic lives in
    forensic-common.ps1, which is dot-sourced below. This file adds the
    three console-specific keyword arrays (vision aimbots, HID emulators,
    capture-card software) and supplies the console-mode verdict tiers:
      - MITM Cheat Stack Detected : vision aimbot or input-adapter
                                    configurator present at HIGH
      - Capture Stack Present     : capture-card or HID-emulator software
                                    found alone (likely a legit streamer)
      - Unsure                    : other MEDIUM matches
      - Clean                     : no HIGH and no MEDIUM matches

.NOTES
    Author: Bread
    Contributor: Drownmw
#>

[CmdletBinding()]
param(
    [string]$OutputPath,
    [switch]$SkipLOLDrivers,    # opt-out of the LOLDrivers (loldrivers.io) network fetch
    [switch]$FetchLOLDrivers,   # opt-IN without prompting (launcher asks once up-front)
    [switch]$SkipBrowserOpen    # don't auto-open the HTML companion at end (unified
                                # launcher passes this so we don't spam two tabs)
)

# ============================================================================
# Output path resolution (handles OneDrive Desktop redirection)
# ============================================================================
if (-not $OutputPath) {
    $stamp = Get-Date -Format 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss'
    $desktop = [Environment]::GetFolderPath('Desktop')
    if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($desktop) -or -not (Test-Path $desktop)) {
        $candidates = @(
            (Join-Path $env:OneDrive 'Desktop'),
            (Join-Path $env:OneDriveConsumer 'Desktop'),
            (Join-Path $env:OneDriveCommercial 'Desktop'),
            (Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE 'Desktop'),
            $env:USERPROFILE
        ) | Where-Object { $_ -and (Test-Path $_) }
        if ($candidates.Count -gt 0) { $desktop = $candidates[0] }
        else {
            $desktop = $env:USERPROFILE
            try { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $desktop | Out-Null } catch {}
        }
    }
    $OutputPath = Join-Path $desktop "AlibiRigReport_${stamp}.txt"
}
$parent = Split-Path -Parent $OutputPath
if ($parent -and -not (Test-Path $parent)) {
    try { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path $parent | Out-Null }
    catch { $OutputPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ([System.IO.Path]::GetFileName($OutputPath)) }
}

# ============================================================================
# CONSOLE-SPECIFIC keyword extras (not in forensic-common.ps1)
# ============================================================================

# Vision-aimbot software: PC apps that watch a capture-card feed and inject
# input. The defining tool of the console-MITM cheat stack. HIGH cheat.
$VisionAimbots = @(
    'aimmmo','aim mmo','aim_mmo',
    'aimsync','aim sync','aim_sync',
    'aimflux','aim flux','aim_flux',
    'norecoilz','no recoil z','no_recoil_z',
    'divisionx','division x','division_x',
    'predator aim','predatoraim','predator-aim',
    'looplus','loop plus','loop+',
    'apox aim','apoxaim','apox-aim',
    'aimkey','aim key',
    'aimx','aim-x','aim_x',
    'kernaim','kernel aim','kernel-aim',
    'colorbot','color bot','color-bot',
    'pixelbot','pixel bot','pixel-bot',
    'ml aim','ml-aim','machine learning aim',
    'ai aimbot','ai-aimbot','ai_aimbot',
    'vision aimbot','vision-aimbot',
    'screen aimbot','screen-aimbot'
)

# HID emulation drivers: make a PC pretend to be a controller. Dual-use
# (Steam + DS4Windows use them legitimately) - MEDIUM, but also a component
# of the console-MITM cheat stack.
$HidEmulators = @(
    'vigembus','vigem bus','vigem-bus','vigemclient',
    'vjoy','v-joy',
    'scptoolkit','scp toolkit','scp-toolkit',
    'ds4windows','ds4 windows'
)

# Capture-card software: dual-use (streamers vs. MITM rigs). MEDIUM alone.
# The verdict logic uses it to choose between CAPTURE STACK PRESENT and
# UNSURE - capture-card-only is a separate, less-suspicious tier.
$CaptureCardSoftware = @(
    'elgato game capture','elgato 4k capture','elgato_gamecapture','gamecapture',
    'avermedia','recentral','rec central',
    'obs studio','obs-studio','obs64','obs32',
    'streamlabs','streamlabs obs',
    'xsplit','x-split',
    'magewell'
)

# ============================================================================
# Initialize shared state and load engine
# ============================================================================
$Findings = [System.Collections.Generic.List[pscustomobject]]::new()

# Dot-source the shared engine.
. "$PSScriptRoot\forensic-common.ps1"

# Console-mode composite keyword arrays. Extends PC base with the three
# console-specific lists above. v3.8 pulls in the new game-specific brand
# arrays + PC-side AI vision aimbots (relevant on a console-rig PC too).
$Keywords_High_Cheats = $CheatBrands_COD + $Spoofer_Brands + $CheatFeature_Names + $DMA_Indicators + $VisionAimbots `
    + $CheatBrands_CS2 + $CheatBrands_Apex + $CheatBrands_Tarkov + $CheatBrands_Rust `
    + $CheatBrands_R6 + $CheatBrands_MarvelRivals + $VisionAimbot_AI_PC
$Keywords_High_Input  = $InputDevices
$Keywords_Medium      = $DMA_DualUse + $DualUse_Tools + $HidEmulators + $CaptureCardSoftware + $CheatBrands_LowConfidence
$Keywords_ScriptHigh  = $ScriptContent_HighRisk
$Keywords_MouseMacro  = $ScriptContent_MouseMacro

# ============================================================================
# Banner
# ============================================================================
# Don't clear the launcher's "[ 2 of 2 ]" progress context (see forensic-scan.ps1).
if (-not $env:ALIBI_LAUNCHER) { Clear-Host }
Write-Host ''
Write-Host '  Alibi v4.3.0 (console-rig mode)' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host '  =======================' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host ''
Write-Host "  Host:   $env:COMPUTERNAME"
Write-Host "  User:   $env:USERNAME"
if (Test-IsAdmin) { Write-Host '  Admin:  Yes' -ForegroundColor Green }
else { Write-Host '  Admin:  No (run as admin for full coverage)' -ForegroundColor Yellow }
Write-Host ''
Write-Host '  Scanning this PC for console-cheat MITM software.' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
Write-Host '  This will take 30-90 seconds.' -ForegroundColor DarkGray
Write-Host ''

# ============================================================================
# Optional LOLDrivers BYOVD cross-reference (opt-in network call)
# ============================================================================
# Reused by Scan-Drivers from parent scope. Cached for 1h so running PC scan
# + console-rig scan back-to-back only prompts once.
$LOLDb = Resolve-LOLDriversDB -SkipLOLDrivers:$SkipLOLDrivers -FetchLOLDrivers:$FetchLOLDrivers

# ============================================================================
# Run the scan
# ============================================================================
Write-Host '  [Phase 1/3] Cheat trace scan' -ForegroundColor Cyan
Invoke-AllScans

Write-Host ''
Write-Host '  [Phase 2/3] Process snapshot' -ForegroundColor Cyan
$processes = @(Get-ProcessSnapshot)

Write-Host ''
Write-Host '  [Phase 3/3] Service snapshot' -ForegroundColor Cyan
$services = @(Get-ServiceSnapshot)

# ============================================================================
# Recency decay (v3.8) - findings older than $RecencyThresholdDays (180d
# default) stay in the report but get demoted so they don't bump the verdict.
# ============================================================================
Apply-RecencyDecay

# ============================================================================
# Console verdict logic
# Filters: Metadata.RecencyClass -ne 'historical' so demoted-by-age findings
# do not contribute to verdict counts. They remain visible in the report.
# ============================================================================
$highCheats = @($Findings | Where-Object { $_.Severity -eq 'HIGH' -and $_.Kind -eq 'cheat' -and $_.Metadata.RecencyClass -ne 'historical' })
$highInput  = @($Findings | Where-Object { $_.Severity -eq 'HIGH' -and $_.Kind -eq 'input' -and $_.Metadata.RecencyClass -ne 'historical' })
$mediumAny  = @($Findings | Where-Object { $_.Severity -eq 'MEDIUM' -and $_.Metadata.RecencyClass -ne 'historical' })

# Historical findings - logged but excluded from verdict per the >180d rule
$historicalFindings = @($Findings | Where-Object { $_.Metadata.RecencyClass -eq 'historical' })
$historicalHigh     = @($historicalFindings | Where-Object { $_.Metadata.OriginalSeverity -eq 'HIGH' })

$procHighCheats = @($processes | Where-Object { $_.Score -eq 'HIGH' -and $_.Kind -eq 'cheat' })
$procHighInput  = @($processes | Where-Object { $_.Score -eq 'HIGH' -and $_.Kind -eq 'input' })
$procMedium     = @($processes | Where-Object { $_.Score -eq 'MEDIUM' })

$svcHighCheats = @($services | Where-Object { $_.Score -eq 'HIGH' -and $_.Kind -eq 'cheat' })
$svcHighInput  = @($services | Where-Object { $_.Score -eq 'HIGH' -and $_.Kind -eq 'input' })
$svcMedium     = @($services | Where-Object { $_.Score -eq 'MEDIUM' })

$totalCheatHigh = $highCheats.Count + $procHighCheats.Count + $svcHighCheats.Count
$totalInputHigh = $highInput.Count + $procHighInput.Count + $svcHighInput.Count
$totalMedium    = $mediumAny.Count + $procMedium.Count + $svcMedium.Count

# Are MEDIUM hits all from capture-card or HID-emulator lists? If yes,
# that's a legitimate-streamer signal, not a generic "unsure".
function Test-IsCaptureOrHidPattern {
    param([string]$Pattern)
    if (-not $Pattern) { return $false }
    $p = $Pattern.ToLower()
    foreach ($k in ($CaptureCardSoftware + $HidEmulators)) {
        if ($p -match [regex]::Escape($k.ToLower())) { return $true }
    }
    return $false
}

$captureOrHidMediumCount = 0
$otherMediumCount = 0
foreach ($f in $mediumAny) {
    if (Test-IsCaptureOrHidPattern $f.Metadata.Pattern) { $captureOrHidMediumCount++ }
    else { $otherMediumCount++ }
}
foreach ($p in $procMedium) {
    if (Test-IsCaptureOrHidPattern $p.Pattern) { $captureOrHidMediumCount++ }
    else { $otherMediumCount++ }
}
foreach ($s in $svcMedium) {
    if (Test-IsCaptureOrHidPattern $s.Pattern) { $captureOrHidMediumCount++ }
    else { $otherMediumCount++ }
}

$verdict = if ($totalCheatHigh -gt 0 -or $totalInputHigh -gt 0) { 'MITM CHEAT STACK DETECTED' }
           elseif ($captureOrHidMediumCount -gt 0 -and $otherMediumCount -eq 0) { 'CAPTURE STACK PRESENT' }
           elseif ($totalMedium -gt 0) { 'UNSURE' }
           else { 'CLEAN' }

$namedCheats = Get-Named-Items $Findings $processes $services 'cheat' 'HIGH'
$namedInput  = Get-Named-Items $Findings $processes $services 'input' 'HIGH'

# ============================================================================
# Compose output file
# ============================================================================
$lines = [System.Collections.Generic.List[string]]::new()

# QUICK READ block
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('  QUICK READ - START HERE')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add("  VERDICT: $verdict")
$lines.Add('')

switch ($verdict) {
    'MITM CHEAT STACK DETECTED' {
        $lines.Add('  This scan found HIGH-confidence indicators that this PC is part')
        $lines.Add('  of a console-MITM cheat stack. One or more of these is present:')
        $lines.Add('    - Vision-aimbot software (watches capture-card feed, auto-aims)')
        $lines.Add('    - Input-adapter configurator (XIM, Cronus, ReaSnow, KMBox,')
        $lines.Add('      Titan, reWASD - the PC-side software for the hardware')
        $lines.Add('      adapters that translate mouse+keyboard into console input)')
        $lines.Add('    - Traditional PC cheat brands or DMA-cheat artifacts')
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  On a PC connected to a console rig, none of these has a')
        $lines.Add('  legitimate purpose. The report below names exactly what was')
        $lines.Add('  found and where.')
        $lines.Add('')
        if ($namedCheats.Count -gt 0) {
            $lines.Add('  Named items (aimbot / cheat-confidence):')
            foreach ($n in $namedCheats) { $lines.Add("    - $n") }
            $lines.Add('')
        }
        if ($namedInput.Count -gt 0) {
            $lines.Add('  Named items (input-adapter configurator software):')
            foreach ($n in $namedInput) { $lines.Add("    - $n") }
        }
    }
    'CAPTURE STACK PRESENT' {
        $lines.Add('  No vision-aimbot software, input-adapter configurator, or')
        $lines.Add('  traditional PC cheats were detected. However, this scan found')
        $lines.Add('  capture-card software and/or HID-emulation drivers.')
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  These have legitimate uses (streaming, recording, controller')
        $lines.Add('  remapping via Steam or DS4Windows). They are disclosed here')
        $lines.Add('  because they are also components of console-MITM cheat stacks.')
        $lines.Add('  Their presence alone is not evidence of cheating.')
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  Reviewer note: if you are auditing for cheat behavior, the')
        $lines.Add('  absence of any aimbot or adapter software alongside the')
        $lines.Add('  capture-card stack is the relevant finding.')
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  Named items:')
        foreach ($f in $mediumAny) {
            if ($f.Metadata.Pattern) {
                $lines.Add("    - [$($f.Category)] $($f.Metadata.Pattern) - $($f.Detail)")
            }
        }
        foreach ($p in $procMedium) {
            $lines.Add("    - [Process] $($p.Pattern) - $($p.Name) (PID $($p.ProcessId))")
        }
        foreach ($s in $svcMedium) {
            $lines.Add("    - [Service] $($s.Pattern) - $($s.Name) ($($s.State))")
        }
    }
    'UNSURE' {
        $lines.Add('  No HIGH-confidence cheat or input-device matches were detected.')
        $lines.Add("  However, $totalMedium MEDIUM finding(s) require human review.")
        $lines.Add('  These are typically dual-use tools or binaries running from')
        $lines.Add('  user-writable locations that the allowlist does not recognize.')
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  AI HANDOFF: paste the full contents of this .txt file into')
        $lines.Add('  any AI chat (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) and ask it to classify')
        $lines.Add('  each MEDIUM finding as benign / worth-reviewing / suspicious')
        $lines.Add('  with cited sources.')
    }
    'CLEAN' {
        $lines.Add('  No RECENT HIGH or MEDIUM matches against the cheat / input-device /')
        $lines.Add('  dual-use keyword database (within the last ' + $RecencyThresholdDays + ' days).')
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  Scope of this scan:')
        $lines.Add("    Cheat-trace findings checked : $($Findings.Count) total artifacts")
        $lines.Add("    Running processes scored     : $($processes.Count)")
        $lines.Add("    Services scored              : $($services.Count)")
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add('  This is necessary but not sufficient evidence. See limitations')
        $lines.Add('  section at the bottom of this report.')
    }
}

# v3.8: historical findings (>180d) - always surfaced in QUICK READ, but
# AFTER the verdict block so they don't get conflated with current evidence.
if ($historicalFindings.Count -gt 0) {
    $lines.Add('')
    $lines.Add('  ----------------------------------------------------------------')
    $lines.Add("  HISTORICAL findings (logged, did NOT affect verdict)")
    $lines.Add('  ----------------------------------------------------------------')
    $lines.Add("  $($historicalFindings.Count) finding(s) older than $RecencyThresholdDays days were demoted by the")
    $lines.Add('  recency-decay rule. These are visible below in the full report but')
    $lines.Add('  did not count toward the verdict above.')
    if ($historicalHigh.Count -gt 0) {
        $lines.Add('')
        $lines.Add("  Of these, $($historicalHigh.Count) were originally HIGH-severity cheat or input matches.")
    }
}

$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')

# Standard report body
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('  ALIBI v4.3.0 (CONSOLE-RIG MODE) - CONSOLIDATED REPORT')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add("  Generated:  $(Get-Date)")
$lines.Add("  Hostname:   $env:COMPUTERNAME")
$lines.Add("  Username:   $env:USERNAME")
$lines.Add("  OS:         $([System.Environment]::OSVersion.VersionString)")
$lines.Add("  Admin mode: $(Test-IsAdmin)")
$lines.Add("  Verdict:    $verdict")
$lines.Add('')
$netLine = if ($LOLDb) {
    '  Read-only scan. No system state was modified. One outbound network call (loldrivers.io, opt-in).'
} else {
    '  Read-only scan. No system state was modified. No network calls.'
}
$lines.Add($netLine)
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')

# Section 1 - split into RECENT (verdict-contributing) and HISTORICAL
# (logged-only) per v3.8 recency-decay rule.
$recentFindings     = @($Findings | Where-Object { $_.Metadata.RecencyClass -ne 'historical' })
$historicalSection1 = @($Findings | Where-Object { $_.Metadata.RecencyClass -eq 'historical' })

$high = @($recentFindings | Where-Object Severity -eq 'HIGH')
$medium = @($recentFindings | Where-Object Severity -eq 'MEDIUM')
$warn = @($recentFindings | Where-Object Severity -eq 'WARN')
$info = @($recentFindings | Where-Object Severity -eq 'INFO')

$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('  SECTION 1 OF 3 - CHEAT TRACE SCAN')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add("  Summary (recent, within last $RecencyThresholdDays days - verdict-relevant):")
$lines.Add("    HIGH    findings : $($high.Count)")
$lines.Add("    MEDIUM  findings : $($medium.Count)")
$lines.Add("    INFO    items    : $($info.Count)")
$lines.Add("    WARN    (access) : $($warn.Count)")
if ($historicalSection1.Count -gt 0) {
    $lines.Add('')
    $lines.Add("  Summary (historical, >$RecencyThresholdDays days old - logged but did NOT affect verdict):")
    $lines.Add("    Demoted historical findings : $($historicalSection1.Count)")
    $lines.Add("    (Originally HIGH-severity   : $(($historicalSection1 | Where-Object { $_.Metadata.OriginalSeverity -eq 'HIGH' }).Count))")
}
$lines.Add('')

if ($Findings.Count -eq 0) {
    $lines.Add('  No findings.'); $lines.Add('')
} else {
    foreach ($f in ($recentFindings | Sort-Object @{E={
        switch ($_.Severity) { 'HIGH' {1} 'MEDIUM' {2} 'WARN' {3} 'INFO' {4} default {5} }
    }}, Category)) {
        $lines.Add("  [$($f.Severity)/$($f.Kind)] [$($f.Category)] $($f.Detail)")
        $lines.Add("        Source: $($f.Source)")
        foreach ($k in $f.Metadata.Keys) {
            $v = $f.Metadata[$k]
            if ($v -ne $null -and $v -ne '') { $lines.Add("        $k`: $v") }
        }
        $lines.Add('')
    }

    if ($historicalSection1.Count -gt 0) {
        $lines.Add('  ------------------------------------------------------------')
        $lines.Add("  HISTORICAL FINDINGS (>$RecencyThresholdDays days old, did NOT affect verdict)")
        $lines.Add('  ------------------------------------------------------------')
        $lines.Add('')
        foreach ($f in ($historicalSection1 | Sort-Object @{E={ -([int]$_.Metadata.AgeDays) }}, Category)) {
            $orig = if ($f.Metadata.OriginalSeverity) { "was $($f.Metadata.OriginalSeverity)" } else { 'demoted' }
            $age = if ($f.Metadata.AgeDays) { "$($f.Metadata.AgeDays)d old" } else { 'age unknown' }
            $lines.Add("  [$($f.Severity)/$($f.Kind)] [$($f.Category)] [HISTORICAL $orig, $age] $($f.Detail)")
            $lines.Add("        Source: $($f.Source)")
            foreach ($k in $f.Metadata.Keys) {
                $v = $f.Metadata[$k]
                if ($v -ne $null -and $v -ne '') { $lines.Add("        $k`: $v") }
            }
            $lines.Add('')
        }
    }
}

# Section 2
$procHigh = @($processes | Where-Object Score -eq 'HIGH')
$procMed = @($processes | Where-Object Score -eq 'MEDIUM')
$procLow = @($processes | Where-Object Score -eq 'LOW')
$procClean = @($processes | Where-Object Score -eq 'CLEAN')

$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('  SECTION 2 OF 3 - RUNNING PROCESSES (scored)')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add("  Total processes captured: $($processes.Count)")
$lines.Add("    HIGH:   $($procHigh.Count)")
$lines.Add("    MEDIUM: $($procMed.Count)")
$lines.Add("    LOW:    $($procLow.Count)")
$lines.Add("    CLEAN:  $($procClean.Count)")
$lines.Add('')

if ($procHigh.Count -gt 0 -or $procMed.Count -gt 0) {
    $lines.Add('  HIGH and MEDIUM processes (full detail):')
    $lines.Add('')
    foreach ($p in (@($procHigh) + @($procMed))) {
        $lines.Add("    [$($p.Score)/$($p.Kind)] $($p.Name) (PID $($p.ProcessId))")
        $lines.Add("        Path:    $($p.ExecutablePath)")
        $lines.Add("        Cmd:     $($p.CommandLine)")
        $lines.Add("        Reason:  $($p.Reason)")
        if ($p.Pattern) { $lines.Add("        Pattern: $($p.Pattern)") }
        $lines.Add('')
    }
}

if ($processes.Count -gt 0) {
    $lines.Add('  Full process table (sorted by suspicion score):')
    $lines.Add('')
    $procTable = $processes | Select-Object Score, ProcessId, ParentProcessId, Name, Started, ExecutablePath, CommandLine |
        Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap | Out-String -Width 500
    $lines.Add($procTable)
}

# Section 3
$svcHigh = @($services | Where-Object Score -eq 'HIGH')
$svcMed = @($services | Where-Object Score -eq 'MEDIUM')
$svcLow = @($services | Where-Object Score -eq 'LOW')
$svcClean = @($services | Where-Object Score -eq 'CLEAN')

$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('  SECTION 3 OF 3 - SERVICES (scored)')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add("  Total services captured: $($services.Count)")
$lines.Add("    HIGH:   $($svcHigh.Count)")
$lines.Add("    MEDIUM: $($svcMed.Count)")
$lines.Add("    LOW:    $($svcLow.Count)")
$lines.Add("    CLEAN:  $($svcClean.Count)")
$lines.Add('')

if ($svcHigh.Count -gt 0 -or $svcMed.Count -gt 0) {
    $lines.Add('  HIGH and MEDIUM services (full detail):')
    $lines.Add('')
    foreach ($s in (@($svcHigh) + @($svcMed))) {
        $lines.Add("    [$($s.Score)/$($s.Kind)] $($s.Name) ($($s.State))")
        $lines.Add("        Display: $($s.DisplayName)")
        $lines.Add("        Path:    $($s.PathName)")
        $lines.Add("        Mode:    $($s.StartMode)")
        $lines.Add("        Reason:  $($s.Reason)")
        if ($s.Pattern) { $lines.Add("        Pattern: $($s.Pattern)") }
        $lines.Add('')
    }
}

if ($services.Count -gt 0) {
    $lines.Add('  Full service table (sorted by suspicion score):')
    $lines.Add('')
    $svcTable = $services | Select-Object Score, Name, DisplayName, State, StartMode, PathName |
        Format-Table -AutoSize -Wrap | Out-String -Width 500
    $lines.Add($svcTable)
}

# Limitations
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('  COVERAGE LIMITATIONS')
$lines.Add('================================================================')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add('  - The CONSOLE itself cannot be scanned. This script can only see')
$lines.Add('    the Windows PC connected to the rig. A pure console + TV setup')
$lines.Add('    with no PC in the loop cannot be audited this way - use the')
$lines.Add('    visual setup checklist (console-setup-checklist.html) instead.')
$lines.Add('  - DMA cheats cannot be detected at runtime by design (no PC-side')
$lines.Add('    footprint). This scan flags DMA development artifacts only.')
$lines.Add('  - Input devices configured on a separate machine and used purely')
$lines.Add('    as pass-through leave no trace on this PC.')
$lines.Add('  - Keyword matching only. Sophisticated cleaners can wipe most')
$lines.Add('    of these artifacts.')
$lines.Add('  - A clean result is necessary but not sufficient.')
$lines.Add('')
$lines.Add("  Report generated: $(Get-Date)")
$lines.Add('')

# Write
$lines | Set-Content -Path $OutputPath -Encoding UTF8

# Console summary
Write-Host ''
Write-Host '  ============================================================' -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host '  Scan complete.' -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ''
$vcolor = switch ($verdict) {
    'MITM CHEAT STACK DETECTED' { 'Red' }
    'CAPTURE STACK PRESENT'     { 'Yellow' }
    'UNSURE'                    { 'Yellow' }
    'CLEAN'                     { 'Green' }
}
Write-Host "  VERDICT: $verdict" -ForegroundColor $vcolor
Write-Host ''
Write-Host "  Cheat HIGH:    $totalCheatHigh"
Write-Host "  Input HIGH:    $totalInputHigh"
Write-Host "  MEDIUM total:  $totalMedium  (capture/HID: $captureOrHidMediumCount, other: $otherMediumCount)"
Write-Host "  Procs scored:  $($processes.Count)"
Write-Host "  Svcs scored:   $($services.Count)"
Write-Host ''
Write-Host "  Saved to: $OutputPath" -ForegroundColor Cyan
Write-Host '  ============================================================' -ForegroundColor Green
Write-Host ''

# ============================================================================
# Auto-generate visual HTML companion (no user prompt)
# ============================================================================
$visualScript = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot 'generate-visual-companion-console.ps1'
$htmlPath = $null
if (Test-Path $visualScript) {
    Write-Host '  Generating visual HTML companion...' -ForegroundColor Cyan
    try {
        & $visualScript -InputPath $OutputPath
        $base = [System.IO.Path]::GetFileNameWithoutExtension($OutputPath)
        $dir  = [System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName($OutputPath)
        $htmlPath = Join-Path $dir "${base}_visual.html"
        if (Test-Path $htmlPath) {
            Write-Host "  HTML visual saved: $htmlPath" -ForegroundColor Cyan
        } else {
            $htmlPath = $null
        }
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  Visual generation failed: $_" -ForegroundColor Red
        $htmlPath = $null
    }
}

if ($htmlPath -and -not $SkipBrowserOpen) {
    # explorer.exe relay so the browser launches at medium IL even when this
    # script is elevated (Start-Process direct can fail to attach to an
    # existing non-admin browser process).
    try {
        Write-Host "  Opening report in your default browser..." -ForegroundColor Cyan
        Start-Process -FilePath 'explorer.exe' -ArgumentList "`"$htmlPath`"" -ErrorAction Stop
    } catch {
        Write-Host "  Could not auto-open the browser. Open this file manually: $htmlPath" -ForegroundColor Yellow
    }
}
Write-Host ''

# Write a tiny summary file the launcher .bat can read to show a
# consolidated end-of-run summary across both scans.
try {
    $sum = "$verdict|$OutputPath|$totalCheatHigh|$totalInputHigh|$totalMedium"
    [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText("$env:TEMP\alibi-console.summary", $sum, (New-Object System.Text.UTF8Encoding($false)))
} catch {}

```

`alibi-engine/scanner/console-run-check.bat`:

```bat
@echo off
REM ========================================================================
REM  Alibi (console-rig mode) - Launcher
REM
REM  Use this if a PC is part of your console gaming rig (capture-card host,
REM  second monitor, streaming PC, etc.) and you want to demonstrate that
REM  PC is not being used to help cheat at console games.
REM
REM  If there is no PC connected to your console, this is not the right
REM  tool - use console-setup-checklist.html instead.
REM
REM  Double-click this file to run the scan.
REM  The scan will request admin permission (UAC prompt) for full coverage.
REM  Output: ONE timestamped .txt file on your Desktop (AlibiRigReport_*.txt).
REM
REM  Author: Bread
REM  Contributor: Drownmw
REM ========================================================================

REM --- Self-elevate if not already admin ----------------------------------
NET SESSION >nul 2>&1
if %errorLevel% NEQ 0 (
    echo.
    echo  Requesting administrator permission for full scan coverage...
    echo  Please approve the UAC prompt.
    echo.
    powershell.exe -NoProfile -Command "Start-Process '%~f0' -Verb RunAs"
    exit /b
)

REM --- We're elevated. Run the scan. --------------------------------------
echo.
echo  Starting Alibi (console-rig mode) (elevated)...
echo.

REM Find console-rig-audit.ps1 next to this .bat (using %~dp0 for portability)
set "SCRIPT=%~dp0console-rig-audit.ps1"

if not exist "%SCRIPT%" (
    echo.
    echo  ERROR: console-rig-audit.ps1 not found in the same folder as this .bat
    echo  Expected: %SCRIPT%
    echo.
    pause
    exit /b 1
)

powershell.exe -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File "%SCRIPT%"

echo.
echo  Press any key to close this window.
pause >nul

```

`alibi-engine/scanner/console-setup-checklist.html`:
